question_area.text: The reading passage has five paragraphs, A–F.
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
1. According to the writer, casual conversation primarily serves to
A) reduce feelings of boredom.
B) establish connections.
C) eliminate structured introductions.
2. What does the study indicate?
A) People derive pleasure from talk.
B) Talk occurs with greater regularity.
C) People experience discomfort.
Do the following statements agree with the passage?
Write TRUE if the statement agrees, FALSE if it contradicts, NOT GIVEN if there is no information.
3. The average person spends approximately two hours per week on small talk.
Complete the sentences below.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
People often check whether the other person seems friendly and ________________.
Conversation generally avoids ________________ or divisive subject matter.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Small talk serves as a social bridge between being a stranger and becoming an acquaintance.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Research shows that the average person spends approximately one hour per week engaging in casual conversation.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Skilled conversationalists tend to use open-ended questions to invite more detailed responses.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Nearly 40% of respondents reported feeling anxious when required to make small talk at networking events.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Small talk conventions vary significantly across different cultures and social contexts.

answer_key.text: 1. B — Paragraphs A and B explain that small talk acts as a social bridge.
2. C — Paragraph D reports that nearly 40% of respondents felt anxious.
3. FALSE — Paragraph B states the average person spends approximately one hour per week, not two.
4. approachable — Paragraph A lists friendly and approachable as key traits.
5. heavy — Paragraph C mentions avoiding heavy or controversial subjects.

passages.title: How We Read Body Language
heading_list: Why isolated gestures can mislead interpreters
The universal language of facial expression
The dominance of visual signals in human interaction
What digital platforms remove from communication
Finding the correct balance of gaze
How manual movements support thinking and expression
The importance of physical space in conversation

question_area.text: The reading passage has six paragraphs, A–F.
Choose the correct heading for paragraphs A–E from the list of headings below.
List of Headings
i. Why isolated gestures can mislead interpreters
ii. The universal language of facial expression
iii. The dominance of visual signals in human interaction
iv. What digital platforms remove from communication
v. Finding the correct balance of gaze
vi. How manual movements support thinking and expression
vii. The importance of physical space in conversation
1. Paragraph A ______
2. Paragraph B ______
3. Paragraph C ______
4. Paragraph D ______
5. Paragraph E ______
Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage.
Experts advise looking for ________________ of non-verbal signals together.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Between 60% and 80% of what we understand comes not from language but from visual signals.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Harvard psychologists found the ideal length for eye contact to be between three and five seconds.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Paul Ekman identified six basic emotions that are recognised across all cultures.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Experts advise looking for clusters of signals rather than interpreting a single gesture.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: People who gesture more are seen as more enthusiastic and expressive.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The same gesture may carry different meanings in different cultural contexts.

answer_key.text: 1. iii — Paragraph A establishes that between 60% and 80% of understanding comes from visual signals.
2. v — Paragraph B is devoted entirely to eye contact and the ideal duration.
3. ii — Paragraph C discusses Paul Ekman’s six basic emotions.
4. i — Paragraph D explains that a single gesture rarely carries a fixed meaning.
5. vi — Paragraph E describes how hand gestures may actually help us think more clearly.
6. clusters — Paragraph D advises looking for clusters of signals.

passages.title: Is Digital Communication Changing Human Connection
heading_list: An experiment measuring social media's effect on wellbeing
Why video calls struggle to build fresh connections
What survey data reveal about friendship
The emotional capacity of written messages

question_area.text: The reading passage has five paragraphs, A–E.
Choose the correct heading for paragraphs A–D from the list of headings below.
List of Headings
i. An experiment measuring social media's effect on wellbeing
ii. Why video calls struggle to build fresh connections
iii. Framing the core disagreement
iv. What survey data reveal about friendship
v. The emotional capacity of written messages
vi. A measured final assessment
1. Paragraph A ______
2. Paragraph B ______
3. Paragraph C ______
4. Paragraph D ______
Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
5. What is the author’s overall conclusion?
A) Digital communication harms relationships.
B) The impact depends on how it is used.
C) Technology always improves connections.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The core disagreement is whether digital tools weaken or strengthen relationships.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Text-based messaging compensates through features such as emojis and spelling variations.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Limiting social media use to ten minutes per day reduced loneliness in some participants.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Video calls were effective for maintaining existing relationships but not for forming new ones.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Those who use technology as a substitute for real interaction are more likely to experience isolation.

answer_key.text: 1. iii — Paragraph A presents the two opposing positions.
2. v — Paragraph B focuses on text-based messaging and emotional nuance.
3. i — Paragraph C describes the controlled experiment.
4. ii — Paragraph D discusses Oxford research on video calls.
5. B — Paragraph F states the impact depends on how it is used.
